name: SiteExpens Mobile Design System
description: Clean, card-driven mobile design system for a Phase-2 interior-fitout project management app. Aesthetic is soft minimalism — airy layouts, periwinkle accent, pill buttons, rounded cards. Information architecture modeled after category-leading competitors (Onsite/Staart/Simple) BUT with a calmer, interior-designer-appropriate visual language — no dark purple headers, no neon pinks, no harsh borders.

designPhilosophy.targetUser: Interior designers and small interior-fitout firm owners in India running 3–15 active sites. They live on mobile, need fast data entry between site visits, and value visual calm over information density.
principles: Content floats on top of the canvas inside rounded 'card' surfaces — the canvas itself is never bare content.
One vivid brand accent (periwinkle / indigo-blue) carries all primary actions, selection states, and brand moments. Everything else is neutral.
Color is used sparingly and with intent: accent = action, semantic = money/status, neutral = content. Never decorative color.
Hierarchy is established by weight and size, not by color or borders. Dividers are hairlines or absent.
Corners are always rounded. Buttons are fully pill-shaped. Cards use 18pt radius.
Shadows are soft, low-opacity, and large-radius — never harsh. Elevation is a whisper.
Every interactive target has breathing room — minimum 44pt tap targets, generous padding inside cards.
Empty states use a single illustration or glyph + short sentence + one CTA. Never walls of text.
Information density is tiered: dashboards show stats + quick actions + scannable list; detail screens use horizontal tabs to avoid burying content.
Money is always right-aligned, tabular, and color-coded (green = incoming/settled, red = outgoing/pending).
notMatching: dark saturated headers (competitors use dark purple — we do NOT)
harsh neon pinks / reds on chrome
gradients as primary surfaces (only as subtle accents on branded hero elements)
screenshot ads / upsell banners on the dashboard

colorSystem.approach: Monochromatic neutrals + one saturated accent + restrained semantic colors. ~80% of any screen is white/near-white, ~10% is neutral text, ~5% is accent, ~5% is semantic (money, status).

primary.role: Primary actions, selected states, links, brand surfaces, focus rings

primaryMuted.role: Tinted backgrounds for icon badges, chip fills, selected-but-subtle states, hero card secondary fill

primaryGradient.role: Hero summary cards (Party Balances, Wallet, etc). Used sparingly — one per screen max.

canvas.role: Screen background — the 'paper' everything sits on

surface.role: Card background, sheet background, default surface

surfaceAlt.role: Subtle inset surfaces (input fields, nested tiles)

border.role: Hairline dividers, input borders, card outlines when needed

borderStrong.role: Emphasized dividers, disabled outlines, unselected chip borders

textMuted.role: Placeholders, disabled text, timestamps, status sub-labels

textOnPrimary.role: Text on top of the primary accent

success.role: Money IN, advance paid, settled, positive deltas

danger.role: Money OUT, to pay, destructive actions, errors

dangerSoft.role: Tinted background for 'Out' / 'To Pay' stat tiles

infoSoft.role: Tinted background for 'Staff Present' / neutral stat tiles

usageRules: Screens use canvas (#F5F6F8) as the root background; cards use surface (#FFFFFF) on top.
Primary buttons are filled with brand.primary and text in textOnPrimary. There are no outlined primary buttons — secondary actions are plain text or ghost pills.
Selected chips, tabs, and list items adopt a brand.primary pill fill with textOnPrimary.
Unselected chips use surface with textSecondary and a 1px borderStrong outline — never filled in a secondary color.
Icons inside cards live inside a brand.primaryMuted tinted rounded square/circle badge with the brand.primary glyph.
Money IN is always semantic.success; money OUT is always semantic.danger. Neutral/settled is textSecondary.
Stat tiles may optionally use the *Soft backgrounds to color-code (mint for in, peach for out, lavender for neutral) — but only in summary strips, never in list rows.
Destructive actions (Delete Project, End Session) are the only use of pure semantic.danger on text.

fontFamily.primary: Inter, SF Pro Text, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif

titleLarge.usage: Nav bar titles, card titles, list item primary text

titleMedium.usage: Section headers, emphasized row labels, amount values in lists

bodyMedium.usage: Default UI text, list secondary lines

label.usage: Button labels, tab labels, form field labels

caption.usage: Metadata, badge text, chip text, timestamps, stat-tile labels

micro.usage: Tab bar labels, uppercase section headers

rules: Screen titles are always centered in the nav bar and paired with a circular back-button on the left.
Large-title headers are only used on the top-level tab screens (Projects dashboard, Parties dashboard); inner detail screens use titleLarge centered in the nav bar.
Pair sizes with weights: 600 for titles, 400 for body, 500 for labels/chips. Avoid 700 except for display.
Letter-spacing is slightly negative on titles, neutral on body, slightly positive on captions — this gives the type a 'calm and intentional' rhythm.
Two-line list rows use titleLarge (600) + bodyMedium (textSecondary) beneath.
Amounts (₹) ALWAYS use tabular numerals so stacked columns align. React Native: fontVariant: ['tabular-nums'].
Screen edge inset is 20pt (lg) on all sides.
Cards have 16pt (md) internal padding; list rows inside a card have 12–16pt vertical padding.
Vertical rhythm between stacked cards/sections is 16pt (md).
Between a section header and its first item: 12pt (sm). Between the last item and the next section header: 24pt (xl).
Primary CTA buttons sit at the bottom of the screen with 20pt horizontal inset and 16–20pt safe-area bottom margin.
Icon-to-label horizontal gap inside a row: 12pt (sm).
Bottom tab bar clears floating content with 80pt content bottom padding.
Never crowd: if in doubt, add 4pt.
Cards: lg (18) — consistently throughout the app.
Primary buttons and chips: pill — always fully rounded, never sharp.
Input fields: md (14).
Icon badges (the tinted squares behind glyphs): md (14) for square, pill for circular.
Profile avatars and circular action buttons: pill.
Stat tiles: md (14) — smaller than cards so they read as sub-elements.
Images inside cards: md (14) — matching input radius so they feel like siblings.

elevation.approach: Shadows are soft, diffuse, and almost imperceptible. They exist to separate the card from the canvas, not to demand attention.

sm.usage: Floating tiles, nav bar, tab bar, sticky action bar

brand.usage: Primary CTA buttons get a tinted drop-shadow in their own brand color

iconography.style: Outlined, rounded, 2px stroke. Monochrome. Sized in 20/24 grid.
rules: Icons in list items live inside a tinted rounded square (brand.primaryMuted fill, md radius, 40×40 or 48×48). The glyph inside is brand.primary.
Transaction list row icons use semantic tint: In → successSoft with success glyph, Out → dangerSoft with danger glyph.
Action icons in the nav bar (back arrow, search, more) are placed inside a 40pt circular surface-white button with a soft shadow (elevation.xs).
Tab bar icons are outline-only, 24pt. The selected tab shows a brand.primary filled glyph + brand.primary label; unselected is textMuted.
Never mix filled and outlined icons in the same context.

primaryNav.notes: Exactly 4 tabs. More would crowd. Side drawer (hamburger) holds org-level settings (Roles, Payroll, Company, Backup).

sideDrawer.trigger: hamburger icon in the top-left of the Projects tab nav bar
sideDrawer.header: org logo/initial + org name + edit icon

projectDetailTabs.notes: Horizontally scrollable. Default tab is Transaction (most-used day-to-day). Tab header stays pinned while the body scrolls.

screen.layout: SafeArea → NavBar → Scrollable content (cards, sections) → optional pinned StickyActionBar → optional BottomTabBar.

navBar.layout: Left: 40×40 circular button (back arrow OR hamburger for top-level tabs). Center: title (titleLarge) OR large-title left-aligned. Right: optional 40×40 circular action button (search/notifications/more).

variants.stacked: Used on pushed screens. Back button left, title centered, action right.
variants.largeTitle: Used on top-level tab screens. Small org-name caption above a large-title heading, avatar button on the right.

navBar.notes: No bottom border. No elevation. Never tinted — sits on canvas.

largeHeader.layout: Left: two lines (uppercase org name in caption textMuted, then displayLarge title in textPrimary). Right: 40pt circular avatar button with company initial (primary fill).
largeHeader.usedOn: Projects tab, Parties tab, CRM tab, Chats tab

variants.default: White surface with tiny shadow, used for list items and panels.
variants.brandHero: Gradient fill (brand.primaryGradient), white text, elevation.lg, 20pt padding. Used for summaryHeroCard only.
variants.imageLed: Card where a photo occupies the left ~30% (or fills the top half); right side / below stacks the title + metadata rows. Used for project list cards.

projectCard.pattern: imageLed card + name row + address row + date range + 3-cell money footer
structure: Top: photo (120pt tall, full card width, radius lg on top corners only) OR 80×80 square on left for compact variant
Title row: project name (titleLarge, 1 line) + trailing kebab menu (3-dot button)
Subtitle row: site address (bodyMedium textSecondary, 1 line, ellipsis)
Footer: 3-cell row — VALUE (primary, titleMedium), IN (success, titleMedium), OUT (danger, titleMedium). Each cell has a caption label above.
projectCard.rightChevron: subtle textMuted chevron if the whole card is tappable

summaryHeroCard.pattern: gradient wide card with 1-3 metric cells and a subtle decorative illustration at the right edge
examples: PARTY BALANCES — two cells: To Pay / To Receive
WALLET — three cells: Balance / In / Out
summaryHeroCard.notes: Title is a caption in 70%-white. Metric values are displayMedium in pure white. Optional right-edge decorative icon (coins stack, wallet, chart) in 70%-white overlay.

statStrip.pattern: horizontal row of 2–3 stat tiles inside a card, used to summarize a section

tile.layout: label (caption textMuted uppercase) above a large value (titleMedium textPrimary). Optional right-side tiny glyph.

examples: Projects dashboard header: Approvals / Material / Tasks pending
Site tab: Site Staff Present / Material Received / Material Used
Transactions tab: Balance / Total In / Total Out / Invoice / Total Expense (2-row 3-col grid)
Task tab: Not Started / Ongoing / Progress%
statStrip.rule: Never more than 3 cells per row. If you need more, stack rows with 8pt gap.

segmentedChips.pattern: horizontal row of outlined pill chips for filtering content (e.g. Inventory / Request / Received / Used)
segmentedChips.distinction: Different from `scrollableTabBar`: chips are for FILTERING a list, tabs are for SWITCHING between content sections.
examples: Attendance filter: All / Site Staff / Labour Contractor
Material filter: Inventory / Request / Received / Used
Design filter: 2D Layout / 3D Layout / Production Files

scrollableTabBar.pattern: horizontal row of text tabs with an animated underline indicator, used inside a detail screen to switch between content sections
scrollableTabBar.layout: Horizontally scrollable list of labels. Active label has a 3pt brand.primary underline pill centered beneath, rounded top. Height 44pt.
scrollableTabBar.borderBottom: none (use 16pt spacing to the content instead)
scrollableTabBar.notes: Tabs do NOT take the dark header chrome competitors use. They sit on the white surface under the nav bar, separated by 8pt.
scrollableTabBar.usedOn: ProjectDetail (Party, Transaction, Site, Task, Attendance, Material, MOM, Design, Files)

dateNavigator.pattern: ‹ [date pill] › — used on Site, Attendance and any date-scoped tab
dateNavigator.layout: Left arrow button (32pt circle surface elevation xs), center date pill (primary tint fill, caption uppercase day name over titleMedium day number), right arrow button
dateNavigator.alternatePattern: Single-line scrollable strip of 7 day-chips centered on today, like an iOS date picker.

stickyActionBar.pattern: pinned to the bottom of the screen, above the safe-area, holds primary action(s) for the current tab

dualCTA.content: Two full-width buttons side by side with 8pt gap — left and right use different semantic fills (success + danger for Payment In/Out; primary muted + primary fill for Request/Received)
dualCTA.note: Optional center 56pt FAB sitting on top of the seam — taps open a generic create sheet

stickyActionBar.rule: Sticky action bar replaces floating FABs on tabs that have clear primary actions. On tabs with multiple equal actions (Projects list), keep a floating FAB instead.

bottomTabBar.itemLayout: Icon (24pt outline) above label (micro). Selected item has brand.primary filled icon + brand.primary label; unselected is textMuted.
bottomTabBar.notes: 4 tabs exactly: Projects, Parties, CRM, Chats. Icons are outline-only, same style.

primary.disabled: background = brand.primaryMuted, label = textMuted, no shadow
primary.layout: Almost always full-width with 20pt horizontal screen inset.

success.usage: Payment In button in dual sticky action bar

danger.usage: Payment Out button in dual sticky action bar

iconCircular.usage: Back buttons, nav actions, drawer hamburger, date navigator arrows

destructiveText.style: plain-text row with danger color, leading trash icon
destructiveText.usage: Delete Project row at bottom of Project Settings

chip.layout: Horizontal scrollable row with 8pt gaps. First chip is usually 'All'.
chip.notes: Chips are for mutually-exclusive mode switching inside a tab. For filter-style mode, use segmentedChips (outlined) instead.

searchField.notes: When inline in a toolbar row, drop the leading icon and put it trailing instead.

toolbarRow.pattern: single row of small action buttons + optional inline search/filter controls, sits above a list
toolbarRow.layout: Left: icon buttons (filter, sort, search) — 36pt squares with subtle surface + border. Right: primary inline action (+ New Party / + Project) in brand.primary text.

listRow.layout: [leading icon-badge or avatar 40×40] — [title (titleLarge) + subtitle (bodyMedium textSecondary)] — [trailing: amount+status OR price OR chevron OR radio]
listRow.dividers: none inside a card; between cards use 16pt spacing instead
listRow.tappable: entire row, pressed state = surfaceAlt fill

partyRow.leading: 40pt circular avatar with initial (colored fill — primary, success, warning, or info — hashed from the party id)
partyRow.body: name (titleMedium) + role subtitle (bodyMedium textSecondary — e.g. 'Material Supplier', 'Staff', 'Client')

amountStatus.pattern: right-aligned money value with a tiny status label underneath
amountStatus.layout: Top line: amount in titleMedium, color-coded (success for incoming/advance paid, danger for to pay, textSecondary for settled). Bottom line: tiny caption textMuted label ('Advance Paid', 'To Pay', 'Settled', 'I Have Advance').
amountStatus.rule: Amount and status ALWAYS colored as a pair — they must agree.

transactionRow.leading: 40pt rounded square tile — successSoft fill with success up-arrow (In) OR dangerSoft fill with danger down-arrow (Out)
transactionRow.body: top line: date + type ('08 Apr 26, Payment Out') in caption textMuted. Main line: party name (titleMedium). Optional subtitle: description (bodyMedium textSecondary).
transactionRow.trailing: amount in titleMedium, color-coded same as the leading tile
transactionRow.dividers: 1px border separator between rows inside the list card

sectionHeaderInlineAction.pattern: section label on the left + colored action text on the right, used above a list inside a card
sectionHeaderInlineAction.layout: Left: label (titleMedium textPrimary). Right: action (label brand.primary, e.g. '+ Add Material', '+ Add Site Staff', '+ New Party', 'View All ›')
sectionHeaderInlineAction.rule: Use for in-card actions that don't warrant a sticky button.

trustCard.pattern: two-row card with icon + title + subtitle each, used to communicate assurance
rows: [green shield glyph] 100% Safe — Onsite is safe
[green database glyph] 100% Auto Data Backup — All data is linked to your phone number

memberRow.pattern: list row for a team member with a role badge
memberRow.variant: In Project Settings, this row sits inside a card with a 'Project Access N Members' header and a 'View All' footer link.

destructiveRow.pattern: card row with a trash icon on the left and 'Delete Project' (or similar) in semantic.danger on the right
destructiveRow.layout: [trash glyph danger] — label (titleMedium danger). Right-aligned chevron optional.
destructiveRow.confirmation: Tapping opens a confirmation bottom sheet with a danger primary button and a cancel secondary.

drawerNav.pattern: side drawer (slide from left) with org header, nav list, demo upsell, and version footer
drawerNav.header: [org initial square] + org name + edit icon
drawerNav.footer: app version + terms & conditions link in caption textMuted

fab.usage: Projects list dashboard only. Replaced by stickyActionBar on tabs with dedicated primary actions.

avatar.placeholderFill: deterministic from id hash → primary, success, warning, or info

screenStructure: 1. NavBar (56pt) — large-title on top-level tabs, centered-title on pushed screens.
2. Optional horizontal scrollable chip / scrollableTabBar (44–56pt tall).
3. Scrollable body of cards separated by 16pt.
4. Optional pinned stickyActionBar OR floating FAB.
projectsDashboard: statStrip (Approvals / Material / Tasks pending) — 3 colored tiles
toolbarRow (filter, sort, search) + inline '+ Project' action
FAB (+ in the bottom-right, above the bottom tab bar)
projectDetail: navBar (back, project name centered, settings kebab right)
scrollableTabBar (Party, Transaction, Site, Task, Attendance, Material, MOM, Design, Files)
tab body — empty state OR its own structure (see per-tab patterns)
transactionTab: statStrip (Balance / Total In / Total Out + Invoice / Total Expense — 2-row 3-col grid)
transaction list (transactionRow inside one card with dividers)
partyTabInProject: Team Members row with 'Manage Access ›' link
party list (partyRow inside a single card)
partiesGlobal: summaryHeroCard (Party Balances — To Pay / To Receive)
toolbarRow (filter, sort, search, Active, PDF, + New Party)
siteTab: statStrip (Staff Present / Material Received / Material Used)
Site Photos horizontal strip with '+' tile
layoutPatterns.formFlow: Forms present each field as label (above) + input (surfaceAlt fill), stacked with 16pt gaps, scrolled, with a single pinned stickyActionBar.singleCTA at the bottom.
layoutPatterns.successFlow: Success screens center a large illustration or iconic circular check badge, followed by displayLarge headline, one line of bodyLarge textSecondary, then a primary CTA and a secondary text action beneath.

phase1_done: Projects: list + create + detail (with empty tab bodies)
phase2_current: Bottom tab bar restructure (Projects / Parties / CRM / Chats)
New UI primitives (StatStrip, SegmentedChips, ScrollableTabBar, DateNavigator, StickyActionBar, SummaryHeroCard)
Projects dashboard: statStrip + new projectCard layout
Project detail: scrollable tab bar with full 9-tab set
phase3_next: Parties feature: types, Firestore rules, list screens (global + project-scoped), create/edit, avatar color hashing
Transactions feature: types, Firestore rules, payment in/out forms (cost code, photo attach, reference), list screens, summary calculations
R2 photo upload via Cloud Function presigned URL (replaces local URI storage)
phase4_later: Site tab: DPR creation, site photos, daily progress
Task tab: tasks with status / member / schedule
Attendance tab: site staff + labour contractor, daily attendance, payroll
Material tab: inventory / requests / received / used
phase5_later: Drawer: Roles & Access, Payroll People, Master Library, Company Settings, Business Card, Invite Friends, Backup

rules: Press feedback: 120ms scale to 0.98 with standard easing.
Screen transitions: 280ms slide-in from right with emphasized easing.
Bottom sheet: 320ms spring from bottom.
List item appearance on mount: stagger of 40ms per item, 240ms fade+rise 8pt.
Tab switch inside scrollableTabBar: 240ms cross-fade of bodies + 200ms underline slide.
Never animate color — always animate opacity, scale, or position.
All icons that are actions must have an accessible label.
Do not convey state with color alone — pair with weight, icon, or label.
Primary buttons must meet 4.5:1 against their fill (our brand.primary + white clears 4.7:1).
Money color coding (green / red) must be paired with a status word ('Advance Paid', 'To Pay') — never color alone.

do: Use white cards on a grey canvas for ALL content grouping.
Use one accent color for actions and selection — nothing else.
Round every corner; pill every button and chip.
Let the layout breathe — whitespace is a design element.
Put primary actions at the bottom of the screen, always full-width.
Use tinted icon badges (muted brand fill + brand glyph) for all list-row icons.
Use circular white elevated icon buttons for navigation actions.
Color-code money with a green/red/neutral triad AND a status label beneath.
Keep stat tiles to max 3 per row — stack if more.
Use segmentedChips (outlined) for filtering, scrollableTabBar (underline) for section switching — never confuse the two.
dont: Don't use borders where a shadow or spacing will do.
Don't stack more than 2 lines of text in a list row.
Don't let a card touch another card — always 16pt of canvas between them.
Don't use harsh drop shadows — elevation should be a whisper.
Don't use color to convey meaning without a secondary cue (icon/label).
Don't copy the competitor's dark purple header — we live on canvas.
Don't put ads or upsell banners in the main dashboard.
